Completeing the circle.

author: @sasjadv
text:
The reason why people think of energy as a thing is because it' s easy to sell, trade, buy and do that sort of things.
And because people don't think about the term "potential", when talking about potential energy;
whether you see potential energy (lets say from a weight on top of a hill) as energy that's tranfered into kinetic energy, or as purely having the POTENTIAL to make  kinetic energy, you will get the same result.
Just like putting your money into investments, shares, etc. means you don't physically own the money, but people tend to say they have (it' s just in those investments).
Self-diluted managers make the same mistake in education, 5 years of good-4star-rated-education will result in 3 kilograms of intelligence, wich leads to 75% chance of 50liters of wealth. We know this is rediculous, and there are too many reasons to mention why it's wrong.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sasja de vries I don't think that's the case mate - i think it arises because that's how we measure it - what you are talking about is a sociopolitical issues and i am not sure they belong here

replies.author: @sasjadv
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith
I actually totally agree with you, the 'misconception' of energy is indeed caused by the need to measure it. (it's not really a misconception, but let's call it so) And what I thought on this is, is that this 'mistake' is not just limited to science, people make this mistake everywhere.
Therefore my point is that (as I perceive it) the underlying reason we make this mistake is not scientific. A lot of scientific work is product related and sometimes biased by commercial needs, either through time-pressure or rules made up by management.

author: @timbrereton1896
text:
Insightful viewpoints Robert. Great series of videos you have done, I commend you. You have hit at a complex subject that cannot be dealt with in 3 sentences.

I was trained in energy auditing and efficiency to view energy flow as a system made up of subsystems.  Engineering and Physics are broken down into separate knowledge areas, but the harvesting and use of energy crosses these human artificial boundaries and technologies constantly, that is why companies use cross discipline teams to create innovation because Energy innovations often come from understanding the system as a whole, then how to create new efficiencies in how the subsystems inter relate and work together and minimizing the transformation of forms of energy which has efficiency losses (Light and Heat frictional losses) at each transformation step until it can be used for work.

According to Nicola Tesla's own recollection, He came up with the idea to perfect Alternating current and functional AC electric motors while viewing the Sun one afternoon. Pondering the Fusion Reactor electrical generator  Sun and the way its energy radiates out to the planets, He suddenly realized the Solar system was an electric dynamo natural system sending wireless energy to the planets and the planets acting as electromagnetic motors with the planet's iron cores. Gravity is induced and is the result of the movement and flow of electrical charge from the Sun to a planet's iron core. The natural electric motors of the planets then turned on their own axis and also circled the sun in a vortex cycle.  He reverse engineered the solar system and built a miniature electro-mechanical replica. That AC grid system Tesla and George Westinghouse perfected now today basically powers the modern world. GE Corporation first licensed that AC system then eventually bought George Westinghouse's company and gained ownership of the AC patents.


Robert, your points about equilibrium are the head of the nail I think. My incomplete understanding on this subject is the natural motor we live on called earth has multiple energy subsystems we humans can interact with and harvest through copying and then directly moving the equilibrium point or waiting for the points of equilibrium to move through natural reactions and processes that force stored energy or potential energy to move and seek equilibrium again (Combustion which is actually an electrical process at the root of a chemical process is one example, the collection of photons into electrical charge in a solar panel is another)  Moving the tipping point of equilibrium can induce  potential energy  into movement which wants to naturally return to its state of equilibrium if an efficient pathway can be provided, and mimicking nature is often if not always the most efficient method as you accurately point out. Vortexing motion is observable all the way from the solar systems all the way down into individual cells and atoms. This Vortex (circular) motion is caused or is a side effect of the movement of  energy as it moves from potential or stored energy and goes into kinetic motion and tries to get back into equilibrium (apparently). Reverse engineered, vortex motion can be harvested into direct electricity, or into mechanical energy for work ( ie: Water wheel powering a flour grinder) Even Brownian Movement is actually a vortex movement happening at the smallest known levels of existence or creation depending on your viewpoints.

Volta studied the electric eel and its natural battery-capacitor cells that collected electrical charge from the ocean that recieved electrical charge from the sun. Salt water is a natural battery that stores up electricity. Volta built His Voltaic pile based on mimicking nature by copying the electric cells in the electric eel and creating a non biological replica.He literally opened up an electric eel and begun experimenting, including getting a painful shock himself.

Much of Nicola Tesla's research and technology development was based on replicating or copying natural systems He saw in the world and creating miniature machines or static resonator versions of these natural systems. Nicola Tesla's 1902 patent for a Radiant Energy Collection system was a man made copy-replication of  what is now called the Tesla Schumann Cavity and atmospheric electricity.

I believe that bio engineering or copying natural systems we can observe and test and harvest is the thinking outside the box needed to create the fastest new solutions. The Wright Brothers who were bicycle mechanics, essentially beat the world's experts to patent controlled powered flight  by studying nature (birds wings) then copying them and building their own miniature wind tunnel to test various types of wings for lifting power. They then did the work and experimented with actual manned flight with prototypes. They failed over and over again but kept incorporating the learned lessons and any successes into their next prototype. As you point out Robert, at some point we have to move beyond talking and actually work at it as half of what we learn is by doing it. Gaining actual experience is the key to proving or disproving postulations on how to duplicate the natural processes we see all around us but take for granted. Multi Variable testing leads innovators to breakthroughs as theorizing and postulating is only the start. Best Regards and Thank you for the fantastic videos.

author: @consciousenergies
text:
All things are in motion despite our perception. Sadly, I disagree with you in that energy is in and is all things. I feel our idea of systems is perception imposing itself into the environment. It seems we have yet to fully accept the equilibrium of our condition. Especially in the nature of humans and how we attempt to compartmentalize our energy. All systems fail in some instance of time or geometry of space, to give rise to what we perceive as a "new system". Energy however, is motion, as far as we can see in our perceptions of space. Which then arises the question of time?
 Theology and philosophy are fantastic mental exciters, but its not what we think that's matter, its what we do with our energy. That's no typo by the way ;) 
Bah! What do I know though? Perceive the motion in space uniquely my friend. 
 Cheers to you and yours. ﻿

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Diadon Acs I am not sure you are getting my point mate. Energy isn't a thing at all - it is the process things undergo - that doesn't mean all things are made of energy - that's not it at all. That's like say the legs are made OF walking

replies.author: @TeslaExplorer
replies.text: you break a leg, it makes a sound. if a tree falls in the woods and no ones around to hear it, it doesn't make one to me or you. but for nature it does. nature always has a sine passing thru from a billion points of view. maybe not to us......

replies.author: @consciousenergies
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith, I appreciate your view and the time you took to write. I know the standard definition of energy as work being done, and understand your point in saying its not a thing. I just respectfully disagree, because we have had the unfortunate circumstance to create a paradox of quantifying infinity. I agree halfway when you say that its not a thing, while being all things simultaneously.  
Let me put it this way, some things are energetic by just being. It may look like they are not moving to our senses or cognitive ability. In fact we are on a something spinning 460 Meters a second and traveling roughly 30 kilometers per second through space according to our perception. Not to mention the vibrations that make a rock, a rock. As thats what we like to call them in english. 
That is a system that your analogous rock is a part of. Despite all our attempts to quantify systemically, all we do is interact with an infinite possibility. I am not saying there are not patterns in nature we cant define, but we do influence in definition, as thats energy interacting. What is the influences purpose should be reflected upon before implementation, kind if liking setting in motion two objects, what is the effect on collision? That is what makes us unique, is our conscious energy discovering itself. 

 The reason this topic brings me such  sadness, is because its the very essence of creation, in that consciousness is always expressing itself in form. That is my perspective on energy.  In this expression of form we have humans, believing we can some how reorganize something that helped give us the ability to not only believe, but organize in the first place.

 So do you see the paradox of what is being said in energy being a system? I certainly will agree our energetic system is in need of a redux. There are those who believe otherwise and hold on tightly to there beliefs. I could do the same, but it is in letting go we become happy. 
So I do my best to enjoy the currents that flow. Sometimes I ride them, sometimes they pummel me in the face. I do my best to enjoy them none the less.
 Being aware to possibilities of creation in all its forms, is being able to adapt to the changes. Its something that words have a very difficult time conveying. The only word I could find is love, and that's what I have for you my friend.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Diadon Acs I am not saying energy is a system. I am saying things in relation can be seen as a system and energy is the process that a system undergoes to transition from one state to another

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Diadon Acs you should note the process of transition is very different from 'work being done'

author: @penfoldooo2160
text: In computing we create stateless and stateful systems to which transition systems (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transition_system) can be applied to create things like finite state machines (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Finite-state_machine) which underpin many forms of artificial intelligence.  This way of thinking about energy should resonate strongly with students of computer science and programmers especially.

If you look at the rules statistically a cell has a higher chance of dying then living. So, statistically every cell should die out, yet under certain configurations the system not only sustains itself but expands eg the Glider Gun (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conway%27s_Game_of_Life#/media/File:Gospers_glider_gun.gif)
